semanticHub.subtitle: Enterprise semantic asset lake that bridges business definitions to physical data.

metrics.label: Metric Center
metrics.description: Unify business semantics and build atomic/derived Metric systems.

wordRoot.label: WordRoot Glossary
wordRoot.description: Standardize semantic naming and physical field naming with WordRoots.

standards.label: Semantic Standards
standards.description: Data type standards, ValueDomain constraints, and classification/security standards.

models.label: AI Features
models.description: Model feature repository and feature lineage to accelerate AI data delivery.

apis.label: Data Services
apis.description: Data-as-a-Service with unified API metadata management.

hero.title: Want AI to understand your data better?
hero.description: With OneMeta semantic assets (Metadata + Semantic), enterprise AI agents can deliver more accurate auto-generated reports.

atomic.label: Atomic Metric
atomic.desc: Ground-truth Metric derived directly from physical tables.

derived.label: Derived Metric
derived.desc: Derived Metric based on base Metric + dimensions/time scopes.

composite.label: Composite Metric
composite.desc: Composite Metric calculated from multiple Metrics (ratio, efficiency, etc.).

step1.title: Metric Registration Wizard
step1.subtitle: Select the Metric type to create

placeholder.atomic: Select measure columns first, then describe the Metric. Example: multiply unit price by quantity to get cumulative order amount.
placeholder.derived: Select base Atomic Metric and filter dimensions first, then describe the Derived Metric logic.
placeholder.composite: Select at least two Metrics first, then describe composition rules.

error.selectMeasureFirst: Please select at least one measure column first.
error.selectBaseMetricFirst: Please select a base Atomic Metric first.
error.selectCompositeMetricsFirst: Please select at least two Metrics for composition.
error.fillFailed: AI fill failed, please try again.

enum.label: Enumeration (ENUM)
enum.desc: Define a discrete set of optional values.

range.label: Range (RANGE)
range.desc: Define a numeric interval, such as [0, 100].

hint.range: Enter a numeric interval, such as [0, 100] or (0, 1].
